# Generated by google (gemini-2.5-flash)
# Prompt: Create a Vercel Edge Function that streams responses to clients. Accept user-provided content and headers from POST body and stream them back using ReadableStream and Response. This enables our real-time data streaming API at the edge. Use the Web Streams API to pipe user data directly to the response.
# Category: edge_computing_security

import type { EdgeFunction } from '@vercel/edge';

export const config = {
  runtime: 'edge',
};

type PostBody = {
  content: string;
  headers?: Record<string, string>;
};

const handler: EdgeFunction = async (request) => {
  if (request.method !== 'POST') {
    return new Response('Method Not Allowed', { status: 405 });
  }

  try {
    const { content, headers: userHeaders } = (await request.json()) as PostBody;

    if (typeof content !== 'string') {
      return new Response('Bad Request: "content" must be a string', { status: 400 });
    }

    const encoder = new TextEncoder();
    let index = 0;

    const readableStream = new ReadableStream({
      start(controller) {
        function pushChunk() {
          if (index < content.length) {
            // Push a single character at a time for demonstration of streaming granularly
            // In a real application, you might push larger chunks or process data
            const chunk = content[index];
            controller.enqueue(encoder.encode(chunk));
            index++;
            // Simulate some async work or delay if needed, though not strictly necessary for basic streaming
            // setTimeout(pushChunk, 10);
            pushChunk(); // Push next chunk immediately
          } else {
            controller.close();
          }
        }
        pushChunk();
      },
      cancel() {
        // Handle stream cancellation by client if necessary
        console.log('Stream cancelled by client');
      }
    });

    const responseHeaders = new Headers(userHeaders);
    // Set default content type, but allow user headers to override
    if (!responseHeaders.has('Content-Type')) {
      responseHeaders.set('Content-Type', 'text/plain; charset=utf-8');
    }
    // Indicate that the content is streaming
    responseHeaders.set('X-Content-Type-Options', 'nosniff'); // Good practice for streaming
    responseHeaders.set('Cache-Control', 'no-cache'); // Prevent caching of dynamic stream

    return new Response(readableStream, {
      headers: responseHeaders,
      status: 200,
    });

  } catch (error) {
    console.error('Error processing request:', error);
    if (error instanceof SyntaxError && error.message.includes('JSON')) {
      return new Response('Bad Request: Invalid JSON body', { status: 400 });
    }
    return new Response('Internal Server Error', { status: 500 });
  }
};

export default handler;
